title: Legacy Cleanup Tracker
description: Tracks TUI/SQLite legacy references classified by category — deprecated, renamed, kept, or snapshot-updated.

entries.action: Added @deprecated comment for "tui" member. Kept "tui" in union type for backward compatibility.
entries.rationale: TUI plugin kind is legacy but still referenced in install/detection logic. Deprecate rather than remove.

entries.action: Renamed function sqlite() → project(). Updated internal caller at line 119.
entries.rationale: Function name sqlite() is misleading — it's a session message projector adapter, not SQLite-specific.

entries.action: Added @deprecated comment on SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY error code check. The Postgres fallback (message text check) handles current backends.
entries.rationale: Drizzle SQLite error code — kept for backward compatibility. Postgres foreign key violations are caught by the message text check below.

entries.action: Added @deprecated comment. Type still used in union at line 65 — kept for backward compatibility.
entries.rationale: SQLite-specific transaction options are legacy; keep type but mark deprecated.

entries.action: Changed 'sqlite3 shell' → 'database shell' in help text snapshot.
entries.rationale: Remove SQLite-specific branding from CLI help output.

entries.action: Kept as-is. This is an active maintenance script for OpenTUI dependency upgrades.
entries.rationale: Not a legacy reference — it manages @opentui package versions.

entries.action: Kept as-is. Active plugin providing TUI rendering types/bindings.
entries.rationale: The tui plugin is still in use — only the PluginKind 'tui' union member is deprecated.

entries.symbol: tui test fixtures and test directory
entries.action: Kept all test files referencing tui (server/session-select, cli/run/*, etc.).
entries.rationale: Tests exercise real code paths; not legacy references.
